name: saasflow
title: SaaSFlow
description: Track MRR, browse customers and transactions, and ask AI questions about your SaaSFlow data — without leaving Raycast.
author: alexander.peiniger

commands.name: menu-bar
commands.title: MRR & Cash
commands.description: Show current MRR and cash balance in the menu bar, refreshed every 15 minutes.

preferences.name: mrrType
preferences.title: MRR variant
preferences.description: Which MRR figure to show. CMRR (committed MRR) is the default — it includes every active recurring contract, even when invoicing hasn't started yet. MRR counts only what's currently invoicing.

commands.name: switch-company
commands.title: Switch Company
commands.description: Pick which company other SaaSFlow commands and AI tools act on.

commands.name: sign-out
commands.title: Sign out
commands.description: Clear stored SaaSFlow OAuth tokens and the active-company selection.

tools.name: list-companies
tools.title: List Companies
tools.description: List the companies the signed-in user has access to.

tools.name: get-company
tools.title: Get Company
tools.description: Get details for a specific company (name, settings, plan).

tools.name: list-company-users
tools.title: List Company Users
tools.description: List the users that have access to a SaaSFlow company, with their role and last-seen time.

tools.name: list-transactions
tools.title: List Transactions
tools.description: Browse, filter, or paginate through transactions. Supports limit, offset, and accountId. Use a data-* tool for aggregations.

tools.name: get-transaction
tools.title: Get Transaction
tools.description: Fetch the details for a single transaction by id.

tools.name: list-customers
tools.title: List Customers
tools.description: Browse or list customers. Prefer data-customer-mrr or data-cohorts for revenue analytics across customers.

tools.name: search
tools.title: Search
tools.description: Look something up by name when the exact id is unknown — returns hits across customers, vendors, accounts, and transactions.

tools.name: run-data-batch
tools.title: Run Data Batch
tools.description: Run multiple slice aggregations in one round trip. Prefer a dedicated data-* tool for single aggregations.

tools.name: data-mrr-at-date
tools.title: MRR at Date
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for total MRR (or committed MRR) on a specific date. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read.

tools.name: data-mrr-data
tools.title: MRR Data
tools.description: Use this when the user asks how MRR has changed over time, MRR trend, or monthly recurring revenue history. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read.

tools.name: data-monthly-data
tools.title: Monthly Data
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for month-by-month revenue, churn, new/upgrade/downgrade breakdowns, or movement summaries. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read.

tools.name: data-customer-mrr
tools.title: Customer MRR
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for MRR broken down per customer, or which customers contribute the most MRR. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read, customers:read.

tools.name: data-mrr-change-events
tools.title: MRR Change Events
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for individual MRR change events (new, expansion, contraction, churn) in a period. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read.

tools.name: data-mrr-by-product
tools.title: MRR by Product
tools.description: Use this when the user asks how MRR is distributed across products or plans. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read.

tools.name: data-analytics
tools.title: Analytics
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for summary SaaS metrics (MRR, NRR, GRR, churn, LTV, CAC) over a period. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-revenue-expenses
tools.title: Revenue Expenses
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for revenue vs. expenses (P&L summary) for a period. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-revenue-expenses-bar
tools.title: Revenue Expenses Bar
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for revenue/expenses charted month-over-month as bars. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-revenue-expenses-by-category
tools.title: Revenue Expenses by Category
tools.description: Use this when the user asks which categories drive revenue or expenses over a period. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-top-expense-vendors
tools.title: Top Expense Vendors
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for the top expense vendors over a period. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-account-balance-kpis
tools.title: Account Balance KPIS
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for cash/bank account balance KPIs (current, start, end, change). Requires permissions: balances:read.

tools.name: data-account-balance-by-account
tools.title: Account Balance by Account
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for current balances broken down per bank or cash account. Requires permissions: balances:read.

tools.name: data-balance-time-series
tools.title: Balance Time Series
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for cash/bank balances charted over time. Requires permissions: balances:read.

tools.name: data-balance-by-cash-type
tools.title: Balance by Cash Type
tools.description: Use this when the user asks how balances are split across cash types (operating, reserve, savings, etc.). Requires permissions: balances:read.

tools.name: data-cash-flow-time-series
tools.title: Cash Flow Time Series
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for cash inflows and outflows over time. Requires permissions: transactions:read, balances:read.

tools.name: data-cash-flow-by-category
tools.title: Cash Flow by Category
tools.description: Use this when the user asks which categories drive cash inflows or outflows in a period. Requires permissions: transactions:read.

tools.name: data-profit-loss-data
tools.title: Profit Loss Data
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for a P&L statement (revenue, costs, gross/operating/net profit) for a period. Requires permissions: financials:read.

tools.name: data-period-details
tools.title: Period Details
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for the detailed breakdown of a single period (transactions, categorizations, totals). Requires permissions: transactions:read.

tools.name: data-cohorts
tools.title: Cohorts
tools.description: Use this when the user asks about customer retention or revenue retention by cohort. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read, customers:read.

tools.name: data-customer-monthly-data
tools.title: Customer Monthly Data
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for one customer's month-by-month metrics or activity. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read, customers:read.

tools.name: data-customer-changes
tools.title: Customer Changes
tools.description: Use this when the user asks which customers changed (new/upgraded/downgraded/churned) in a period. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read, customers:read.

tools.name: data-retention-cohort-detail
tools.title: Retention Cohort Detail
tools.description: Use this when the user asks for the customer-level detail behind a specific retention cohort cell. Requires permissions: subscriptions:read, customers:read.

preferences.name: apiBaseUrl
preferences.title: API base URL
preferences.description: Override the SaaSFlow API base URL. Leave blank for production (https://api.saasflow.com).

preferences.name: apiKey
preferences.title: API key (fallback)
preferences.description: Personal API key from the SaaSFlow dashboard. Used when OAuth isn't available; OAuth takes precedence when both are set.

ai.instructions: You help the user inspect their SaaSFlow data. Prefer the most specific tool: list-companies to discover companies, get-company for a single company's metadata. If the user references a company by name, look it up with list-companies first to get its id. Format currency values with the company's reporting currency when present.
